# Team News: Portugal vs. Uzbekistan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Nsidibe Akpan| 22 Jun 2026 18:00

© Iconsport / Zuma

Portugal will be aiming to secure their first win of the 2026 FIFA World Cup when they take on tournament debutants Uzbekistan in Group K on Tuesday in Houston, having been held to a 1-1 draw by the Democratic Republic of Congo in their opening match.

With a potentially decisive clash against Colombia still to come, the Selecao das Quinas will view this encounter as a must-win opportunity to strengthen their qualification hopes.

Victory would not only put Portugal in a strong position ahead of their final group game but could also end Uzbekistan's chances of progressing following their opening day defeat to Colombia, and here, Sports Mole rounds up the

# Portugal vs. Uzbekistan lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group K game in Houston

Kyle Bonn

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Portugal hopes it can rescue its 2026 FIFA World Cup trajectory as the European giants take on Uzbekistan on Tuesday from NRG Stadium in Houston.

Cristiano Ronaldo and the rest of the Selecao attack struggled mightily in their opening-round draw with DR Congo, as they scored off a simple cross from the left just six minutes into the game and tried to sit on that lead, creating little up front until Yoane Wissa equalized just before halftime. With the game level in the second half, they were slow and uninspiring, as Ronaldo and company became more and more frustrated.

Uzbekistan, meanwhile, fell to Colombia by a 3-1 score in its first match, pu

# Portugal vs Uzbekistan Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group K

Portugal vs Uzbekistan clash in Houston for their 2026 World Cup Group K Matchday 2 game. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

Updated on June 23, 2026 3:13AM EST

Share

By:

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

Updated on June 23, 2026 3:13AM EST

World Cup

# Portugal vs Uzbekistan Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group K

Portugal did not get the opener they wanted, held to a stunning 1-1 draw by World Cup debutants DR Congo after Joao Neves had put them ahead. Now they need a win at NRG Stadium in Houston to steady the ship, and Uzbekistan, beaten 3-1 by Colombia in their own first-ever World Cup match, are the opponent. Group K is led by Colombia on three points, with Portugal and DR Congo on one and Uzbekistan still searching for theirs.

Portu

# Portugal XI vs Uzbekistan: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026

Pre-tournament favourites are still searching for first win

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 day ago

COMMENTS

Portugal take on Uzbekistan in search of their first win of the 2026 World Cup.

Roberto Martinez’s side were held to a stunning 1-1 draw by the Democratic Republic of Congo in their opener, with Yoane Wissa snatching a point for the African side in first-half stoppage time.

Joao Neves had opened the scoring with a sixth-minute glancing header, and the midfielder will be prominent once more against Uzbekistan in what many have described as the midfield trio of the tournament.

Neves lines up alongside Paris Saint-Germain club-mate Vitinha, with Manchester United captain and Premier League player of the season Bruno Fernandes ahead of them as the

# Team News: Portugal vs. Uzbekistan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Nsidibe Akpan| 22 Jun 2026 18:00

© Iconsport / Zuma

Portugal will be aiming to secure their first win of the 2026 FIFA World Cup when they take on tournament debutants Uzbekistan in Group K on Tuesday in Houston, having been held to a 1-1 draw by the Democratic Republic of Congo in their opening match.

With a potentially decisive clash against Colombia still to come, the Selecao das Quinas will view this encounter as a must-win opportunity to strengthen their qualification hopes.

# Portugal vs. Uzbekistan lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group K game in Houston

Kyle Bonn

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Portugal hopes it can rescue its 2026 FIFA World Cup trajectory as the European giants take on Uzbekistan on Tuesday from NRG Stadium in Houston.

Cristiano Ronaldo and the rest of the Selecao attack struggled mightily in their opening-round draw with DR Congo, as they scored off a simple cross from the left just six minutes into the game and tried to sit on that lead, creating little up front until Yoane Wissa equalized just before halftime. With the game level in the second half, they were slow and uninspiring, as Ronaldo and company became more and more frustrated.

# Portugal vs Uzbekistan Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group K

Portugal did not get the opener they wanted, held to a stunning 1-1 draw by World Cup debutants DR Congo after Joao Neves had put them ahead. Now they need a win at NRG Stadium in Houston to steady the ship, and Uzbekistan, beaten 3-1 by Colombia in their own first-ever World Cup match, are the opponent. Group K is led by Colombia on three points, with Portugal and DR Congo on one and Uzbekistan still searching for theirs.

COMMENTS

Portugal take on Uzbekistan in search of their first win of the 2026 World Cup.

Roberto Martinez’s side were held to a stunning 1-1 draw by the Democratic Republic of Congo in their opener, with Yoane Wissa snatching a point for the African side in first-half stoppage time.

Joao Neves had opened the scoring with a sixth-minute glancing header, and the midfielder will be prominent once more against Uzbekistan in what many have described as the midfield trio of the tournament.
